question: Which fields belong to global identity?
answer: User: id email(unique) name firstName lastName passwordHash avatarUrl authProviderId platformRole status phone mobile address language timezone dateFormat mustChangePassword lastLoginAt lastLoginIp lastLoginDevice createdAt updatedAt. No organizationId on User.
preliminaryRisk: P1 — global profile/password/status affect all orgs

question: Which fields belong to organization?
answer: OrganizationMembership: id userId organizationId role organizationRoleId roleLabel stationScope department position permissions(JSON) stationIds(JSON) fieldAgentAccess status(ACTIVE|INVITED|SUSPENDED|REMOVED) timestamps. Plus org-scoped prefs: UserAccountPreference UserNotificationPreference.
globalImpact: no (except via global User side effects)
preliminaryRisk: P0 — suspend/password/email paths mutate global User from org UI

question: Can an org admin change global identity data?
answer: YES — updateOrgUser can patch User email name first/last phone mobile address language timezone dateFormat and User.status; changeOrgUserPassword updates User.passwordHash globally.
preliminaryRisk: P0 — Org A admin changes credentials/profile used for Org B

question: Can Org A affect access in Org B?
answer: YES — (1) password reset changes shared credential; (2) status SUSPENDED sets global User.status blocking login/refresh for all orgs; (3) email change rewrites unique login identity; (4) no per-org password.
preliminaryRisk: P0 — tenant isolation broken for credential/lifecycle

question: Is suspension org-scoped or global?
answer: GLOBAL User.status — FE deactivate sends status=SUSPENDED which sets User.status=SUSPENDED and does NOT set membership.status=SUSPENDED (membership remains ACTIVE). UI status badge derives from User.status when membership not INVITED/REMOVED.
evidence: updateOrgUser lines 610-613; mapOrgUser uses USER_STATUS_MAP[u.status]; UsersTab deactivate
preliminaryRisk: P0 — Org A deactivate locks user out of Org B; membership enum SUSPENDED unused by this path

question: Is delete membership removal or global user delete?
answer: Org UI DELETE = removeOrgUser → membership.status=REMOVED (User row kept). MASTER_ADMIN users.delete = hard prisma.user.delete (cascades RefreshToken memberships etc).
evidence: removeOrgUser vs UsersService.delete; UsersTab handleDelete → deleteByOrg
preliminaryRisk: P1 — soft remove retains PII; hard delete orphan risk for historical refs

question: What happens to data responsibility and historical references?
answer: Soft remove keeps User + historical ActivityLog userId references. Hard delete cascades DB FKs with onDelete Cascade/SetNull per relation; no anonymization job. Audit descriptions may still contain emails from older LOGIN events.
evidence: schema relations; ActivityLog; no GDPR erase service for User

question: Can same email map to multiple User rows?
evidence: schema @@unique email; auth.controller login; updateOrgUser email normalize
preliminaryRisk: P2 — good constraint; no verified-email field

question: Are emails uniquely normalized?
answer: YES at write/login paths (lowercase trim). DB unique is case-sensitive by default in Postgres unless citext — reliance on app-layer normalization.
preliminaryRisk: P2 — residual case-collision risk if raw SQL inserts bypass app

question: How are email changes and verifications handled?
answer: Org admin or profile update can set User.email immediately with no verification challenge no confirmation email no verifiedAt field. Account self-service profile patch allow-list is firstName lastName phone mobile — email change is org-admin path primarily.
evidence: UpdateOrgUserDto email; account profile allow-list; no EmailVerification model
preliminaryRisk: P0 — account takeover via email rewrite without proof of control

question: Where do MFA fields live?
answer: NO MFA columns on User or membership. Account DTO exposes twoFactorEnabled/Available passkeysAvailable hardcoded false; security activity returns twoFactorEnabled null.

question: Where do role and station scope live?
answer: Org-only on OrganizationMembership (+ OrganizationRole templates). JWT carries membershipRole+organizationId snapshot. No roleVersion/permissionVersion fields anywhere.
preliminaryRisk: P0 — H3 H10 parallel truths + no version invalidation

question: Multiple memberships per user?
answer: YES — unique (userId organizationId) allows many orgs. Prod snapshot had multiOrgActiveUsers=0 but model supports it.
preliminaryRisk: P0 — H4 non-deterministic selection when multi-org exists

question: Active inactive removed semantics
answer: ACTIVE used for auth. INVITED used for invite provisioning. REMOVED soft-delete from org. SUSPENDED enum exists on MembershipStatus but org deactivate path does not set it — sets User.status instead.
preliminaryRisk: P0 — dual status model (User.status vs Membership.status)

question: Global vs org profile fields?
answer: Global on User: name contact locale. Org-scoped: department position roleLabel station* permissions fieldAgentAccess; prefs defaultStationId defaultLandingPage; notification prefs per category.
evidence: schema User vs Membership vs Preference
globalImpact: org admin can edit global contact fields
preliminaryRisk: P1 — unclear data ownership for DSGVO controllers
